Sharpness Disparity Principle in Transformers for Accelerating Language Model Pre-Training","authors":["Jinbo Wang","Mingze Wang","Zhanpeng Zhou","Junchi Yan","Weinan E","Lei Wu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$10","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.19002","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/0003WZYEW25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/0003WZYEW25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/wang25dl.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心问题是：Transformer 各个模块的优化难度并不一样，但预训练时通常对所有 block 用同一个学习率，这可能白白浪费收敛速度。过去大家会分析全局 sharpness 或调统一 LR schedule，却较少系统讨论 embedding、attention、FFN、norm 这些部件之间持续存在的 sharpness disparity，以及能否据此改写优化器。","importance":"这是一个很有 pretrain 味道的系统+优化方法工作。它的重要性不在于换了个小技巧，而在于给出一个可操作的训练加速原则：不同 block 的局部曲率不同，就不该被同一 LR 约束。若摘要中的近 2× 加速可复现，这会直接影响大模型预训练的最优训练栈选择，尤其对长周期训练和大算力集群非常有意义。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Blockwise Learning Rate：先观察并量化不同 Transformer block 的 sharpness 差异，再把学习率按 block 自适应分配，并集成进 AdamW。它不是另起一种全新优化器，而是在 AdamW 框架上做块级 LR 重参数化，因此工程接入成本相对低。与统一 LR 的做法相比，它承认某些模块处在更尖锐的损失地形中，需要更保守步长，而更平坦的模块可以走得更快。这样设计有效的原因是：统一 LR 通常由最敏感模块决定，结果是大量不那么敏感的参数被迫欠更新，整体训练被慢模块拖住。","experiments":"摘要给出的最关键数字是：在 GPT-2 和 LLaMA 上，集成 Blockwise LR 后相对 vanilla AdamW 能达到近 2× speedup，同时终局 loss 更低。作者还强调 sharpness disparity 早期就出现并持续整个训练过程，这意味着该现象不是训练后期偶然噪声，而是稳定结构特征。摘要没有给出模型具体参数量、训练 token 数、wall-clock 定义、在哪些规模上验证，因此不能补充。即便如此，已有信息足够说明两点：第一，这不是只换来相同 loss 更快，还可能改进最终收敛点；第二，收益建立在 block 间差异稳定存在这一前提上，若架构变化很大，例如 MoE、线性注意力或极强 norm 变体，block 划分和 sharpness 估计方式可能需要重做。","authors_note":"作者包含 Junchi Yan、Weinan E。若作者列表无误，这两位在优化、理论与大模型训练方法上都有持续影响力；但仅凭摘要无法确认具体机构归属。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper starts from a practical optimization mismatch: transformer blocks do not have the same training geometry, yet pretraining usually applies one learning rate policy to all of them. Prior work mostly tuned global schedules or optimizer variants, but rarely exploited persistent sharpness differences across embeddings, attention, feedforward, and normalization blocks.","importance":"This is a pretraining optimization contribution with clear systems relevance. The significance is not just a new heuristic, but a concrete principle for speeding up large-scale training: if different blocks sit in different local curvature regimes, a single LR is unnecessarily conservative. The claimed nearly 2x speedup, if robust, would be highly consequential for long-run foundation model training.","technical_approach":"The method is Blockwise Learning Rate, built on top of AdamW. The authors first identify stable sharpness disparity across transformer building blocks, then assign learning rates according to that disparity. This is not a brand-new optimizer so much as a block-level adaptation of a standard one. The rationale is simple: the global LR is often constrained by the sharpest modules, which leaves flatter modules under-updated. Blockwise LR relaxes that bottleneck.","experiments":"The main quantitative claim in the abstract is a nearly 2x speedup over vanilla AdamW on GPT-2 and LLaMA, along with lower terminal loss. Another important observation is temporal: the sharpness disparity appears early and persists throughout training, suggesting it is a structural property rather than a late-training artifact. The abstract does not report model sizes, token budgets, or the exact speed metric, so no further numbers should be added. A reasonable boundary is that the approach assumes a meaningful and stable block decomposition; architectures like MoE or substantially modified transformer variants may require rethinking how sharpness is measured and how LR is assigned.","authors_note":"The author list includes Junchi Yan and Weinan E, both well known for work on optimization, theory, and large-scale model training. The specific affiliations cannot be confirmed from the provided information alone."},"tags":["sharpness","training-dynamics","optimizer","pretrain-acceleration"],"notable_authors":["Junchi Yan","Weinan E"]},{"id":"2502.01804","title":"Soup-of-Experts: Pretraining Specialist Models via Parameters Averaging","authors":["Pierre Ablin","Angelos Katharopoulos","Skyler Seto","David Grangier"],"institutions":["Apple"],"abstract":"Machine learning models are routinely trained on a mixture of different data domains. Different domain weights yield very different downstream performances. We propose the Soup-of-Experts, a novel architecture that can instantiate a model at test time for any domain weights with minimal computational cost and without re-training the model. Our architecture consists of a bank of expert parameters, which are linearly combined to instantiate one model. We learn the linear combination coefficients as a function of the input domain weights. To train this architecture, we sample random domain weights, instantiate the corresponding model, and backprop through one batch of data sampled with these domain weights. We demonstrate how our approach obtains small specialized models on several language modeling tasks quickly. Soup-of-Experts are particularly appealing when one needs to ship many different specialist models quickly under a model size constraint.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.01804","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/AblinKSG25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/AblinKSG25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/ablin25a.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"多领域数据混合训练 LLM 时，不同的领域配比会导致截然不同的下游表现，但为每种配比单独训练一个模型成本极高。本文提出 Soup-of-Experts (SoE)：训练一组专家参数，在推理时根据目标领域权重线性组合出一个定制模型，无需重新训练。","importance":"方法级贡献，直接面向 pretrain 数据配比问题。核心价值在于将'数据配比→模型'的映射参数化了：训练一次，推理时可以按需实例化任意配比的模型。这比 DoReMi 等方法更灵活——后者优化一个固定配比，而 SoE 支持连续的配比空间。对需要服务多个下游场景的 pretrain 模型有实际意义。","technical_approach":"维护一组专家参数（parameter bank），每个专家对应一个基础方向。推理时，给定目标领域权重向量，通过学到的线性组合系数将专家参数混合成一个模型。训练过程：随机采样领域权重→实例化对应模型→用该权重采样的数据做一步训练→反向传播更新专家参数和组合系数。这本质上是在参数空间做 model soup，但组合系数是领域权重的函数而非固定的。与 MoE 的区别：MoE 是 token 级路由，SoE 是模型级的参数混合。","experiments":"在多个语言建模任务上验证，展示 SoE 能用较小的专家模型在不同领域配比下获得接近单独训练的专用模型的效果。具体模型规模和数字在截断摘要中未完整给出。","authors_note":"作者 Pierre Ablin 和 David Grangier 来自 Apple MLR。Grangier 在 NLP/序列建模领域有长期积累。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Different domain mixtures in LLM pretraining yield very different downstream performance, but training a separate model per mixture is prohibitively expensive. Soup-of-Experts (SoE) trains a bank of expert parameters that can be linearly combined at test time for any target domain weights without retraining.","importance":"Method-level contribution directly addressing pretrain data mixing. The key value is parameterizing the 'domain weights → model' mapping: train once, instantiate any mixture at inference. More flexible than fixed-mixture approaches like DoReMi, as it supports a continuous mixture space.","technical_approach":"Maintains a bank of expert parameters. At inference, given target domain weights, learned linear combination coefficients mix experts into one model. Training: sample random domain weights → instantiate model → train on one batch sampled with those weights → backprop through expert parameters and coefficients. Unlike MoE (token-level routing), SoE does model-level parameter mixing where combination coefficients are a function of domain weights.","experiments":"Demonstrated on multiple language modeling tasks. SoE with small expert models approaches the performance of separately trained specialized models across different domain mixtures. Full model scales and numbers truncated.","authors_note":"Pierre Ablin and David Grangier are from Apple MLR. Grangier has extensive experience in NLP and sequence modeling."},"tags":["moe","pretraining","parameter-averaging","data-mixture"],"notable_authors":["David Grangier"]},{"id":"2502.06861","title":"Design Considerations in Offline Preference-based RL","authors":["Alekh Agarwal","Christoph Dann","Teodor Vanislavov Marinov"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Offline algorithms for Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences (RLHF), which use only a fixed dataset of sampled responses given an input, and preference feedback among these responses, have gained increasing prominence in the literature on aligning language models. In this paper, we study how the different design choices made in methods such as DPO, IPO, SLiC and many variants influence the quality of the learned policy, from a theoretical perspective. Our treatment yields insights into the choices of loss function, the policy which is used to normalize log-likelihoods, and also the role of the data sampling policy. Notably, our results do not rely on the standard reparameterization-style arguments used to motivate some of the algorithms in this family, which allows us to give a unified treatment to a broad class of methods. We also conduct a small empirical study to verify some of the theoretical findings on a standard summarization benchmark.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.06861","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/AgarwalDM25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/AgarwalDM25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/agarwal25a.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["rlhf","offline-rl","preference-learning","dpo"]},{"id":"2501.15781","title":"Large Language Models to Diffusion Finetuning","authors":["Edoardo Cetin","Tianyu Zhao","Yujin Tang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"We propose a new finetuning method to provide pre-trained large language models (LMs) the ability to scale test-time compute through the diffusion framework. By increasing the number of diffusion steps, we show our finetuned models achieve monotonically increasing accuracy, directly translating to improved performance across downstream tasks. Furthermore, our finetuned models can expertly answer questions on specific topics by integrating powerful guidance techniques, and autonomously determine the compute required for a given problem by leveraging adaptive ODE solvers. Our method is universally applicable to any foundation model pre-trained with a cross-entropy loss and does not modify any of its original weights, fully preserving its strong single-step generation capabilities. scaling 规律：哪些设计会先改善感知重建，哪些会真正帮助后续生成器学到更好的分布。预期上的 trade-off 也很明确：更强 tokenizer 往往意味着更高编码成本、更大 latent 带宽，未必在所有部署场景都划算。","experiments":"从摘要可确认的事实有三点。第一，他们训练了基于 ViT 的 tokenizer，并使用远超 ImageNet-1K 中反而更伤信息保真。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles an underexplored bottleneck: visual generators have been scaled aggressively, but the tokenizer upstream of them has usually been left relatively small and fixed. That leaves a basic question unanswered: how tokenizer architecture, capacity, and training data affect both reconstruction quality and downstream generation quality. This matters now because visual tokenization is no longer just preprocessing; it is part of the representation layer in unified multimodal pretraining.","importance":"This is a method-and-systems contribution with direct pretraining relevance. It addresses the representation bottleneck in token-based image and video modeling: if the tokenizer throws away too much information, scaling the generator alone has diminishing returns. For multimodal LM work, the practical message is important: the limiting factor may be the tokenizer, not the decoder.","technical_approach":"The central move is to replace the usual convolutional autoencoder backbone with an enhanced ViT-based tokenizer, ViTok, and then study tokenizer scaling on much larger image and video datasets than ImageNet-1K. The contribution is not merely 'use a ViT'; it is to treat the tokenizer itself as a first-class scalable model and evaluate it jointly on reconstruction and downstream generation. Compared with prior tokenizer papers that often optimize one compression point or one reconstruction metric, this work appears to ask scaling-law style questions: which design choices improve perceptual fidelity, and which ones actually help the downstream generator model the data distribution better. The trade-off is predictable: stronger tokenizers typically cost more compute and may increase latent bandwidth.","experiments":"Three points are clear from the abstract. Tu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$16","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.01651","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/HuTZWT25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/HuTZWT25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/hu25v.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是 transformer 长上下文建模里的两个老问题同时存在：一是自注意力二次复杂度太高，二是预训练时学到的注意模式很难长度外推，通常需要再做更大窗口的 post-training，成本高且不稳。作者的目标是找到一种既能保留远距离信息访问能力，又能用常数窗口运行、还能泛化到远超训练长度的注意力机制。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是方法级，并且和 pretrain 强相关。它提供了一个值得注意的方向：不是继续逼近 full attention，而是用因果检索形成动态上下文，把长距离依赖转化成检索问题。若这一思路成立，对长上下文预训练的启示很直接——训练时不一定非要覆盖目标窗口，只要模型学会了稳定的 chunk-level retrieval，长度外推可能比传统位置插值或窗口扩展更自然。","technical_approach":"作者提出 Grouped Cross Attention（GCA）。核心做法是把输入序列切成 chunks，每个 chunk 在生成时从过去的 chunks 中检索 top-k 相关片段，再用 grouped cross attention 访问这些被检索出的上下文，而不是对整个历史做 full self-attention。和很多直接接外部 retriever 的方法不同，摘要强调他们不是用 off-the-shelf retriever，这很关键：若检索器与语言模型目标脱节，检到的片段常常不利于生成；把检索纳入同一机制，可以让“取什么上下文”和“如何利用上下文”共同适配训练目标。设计取舍也很明显：它把信息访问压缩成 chunk 粒度检索，换来常数窗口和更强长度泛化，但若相关信息过于细碎或跨 chunk 边界，可能会有召回损失。","experiments":"摘要给出了一个很强的主结论：GCA 可以在保持常数 attention window 的同时，泛化到预训练上下文长度的 1000 倍，并继续访问远距离信息。这是论文最值得关注的卖点。它还指出，相比需要在更大窗口上 post-train 的 transformer，GCA 试图直接规避这一步。由于摘要片段被截断，无法确认使用的模型规模、预训练长度、测试长度、baseline（如 full attention、sliding window、RMT、retrieval transformer 等）以及困惑度或下游任务数字。读这篇论文时，最关键的实验点应是：1）1000x 泛化是在合成任务、语言建模还是 needle/QA 任务上成立；2）top-k 检索的召回与质量如何随长度增长变化；3）检索器与语言模型是否联合训练；4）相比更简单的 chunk recurrence 或 external retrieval，收益来自因果检索本身还是 grouped cross attention 结构。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles two long-context problems at once: standard self-attention is quadratic, and transformers often generalize poorly beyond the context lengths seen in training unless they are further trained with larger windows. The goal here is to keep access to distant information while running with a constant attention window and extrapolating far beyond pretraining length.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with direct pretraining relevance. The notable idea is to stop approximating full attention and instead turn long-range dependency access into a causal retrieval problem. If that works, it suggests a different route for long-context pretraining: teach stable chunk-level retrieval rather than forcing the model to attend densely across the full target window.","technical_approach":"The proposed Grouped Cross Attention splits the sequence into chunks, retrieves the top-k relevant past chunks for each current chunk, and attends only to those retrieved contexts. The key distinction from many retrieval-augmented approaches is that the paper does not rely on an off-the-shelf retriever. That matters because a separately trained retriever can be poorly aligned with the language modeling objective. By integrating retrieval into the attention mechanism, the model can learn what to fetch and how to use it together. The trade-off is clear: chunk-level retrieval buys constant-window computation and better extrapolation, but it may miss information when dependencies are highly fragmented or cross chunk boundaries in difficult ways.","experiments":"The strongest claim in the abstract is that GCA can generalize to 1000x the pretraining context length while maintaining access to distant information with a constant attention window. That is the headline result. The method is also explicitly framed as an alternative to expensive post-training with larger windows. The abstract is truncated, so model sizes, training lengths, baselines, and exact language-modeling or downstream numbers cannot be reported reliably. The main things to inspect are where the 1000x claim holds, how retrieval quality scales with length, whether retrieval is jointly learned, and how much of the gain comes from the retrieval formulation versus the grouped cross-attention design It is generally believed that the important foundation of its ICL capability is the induction heads mechanism, which requires at least two layers attention. In order to more efficiently implement the ability of the model's induction, we revisit the induction heads mechanism and proposed a KV shifting attention. We theoretically prove that the KV shifting attention reducing the model's requirements for the depth and width of the induction heads mechanism. Our experimental results demonstrate that KV shifting attention is beneficial to learning induction heads and language modeling, which lead to better performance or faster convergence from toy models to the pre-training models with more than 10 B parameters.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.19574","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/XuWC25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/XuWC25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/xu25ac.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文想解决的是：标准 decode-only Transformer 的 induction 能力通常需要至少两层注意力配合，导致模型要用更深或更宽的结构才能学到有效的 in-context pattern copying。作者认为这不是语言建模目标本身要求的，而是注意力实现 induction 的方式不够直接，因此值得从算子层改。","importance":"这是一个小而硬的建模改动，重要性在于它直指 ICL 常被讨论的一个机械基础——induction heads。若结论成立，它意味着部分上下文学习能力可以通过更合适的 attention 参数化更早出现、以更低深度出现；这对小模型、收敛速度和架构搜索都有启发，但目前更像机制导向的架构改进，不是范式级变化。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 KV shifting attention：通过对 key/value 做位移式构造，让模型更直接表达序列中“匹配前缀后复制后续”的 induction 行为。它不是增加外部记忆，也不是改训练目标，而是把原本需要多层组合实现的模式匹配-转移过程压缩进更高效的注意力形式。作者声称有理论证明，表明该设计降低了实现 induction head 所需的深度和宽度条件；和标准注意力相比，关键差别在于它显式编码了相邻位置或偏移位置之间的转移结构，因此学习难度更低。","experiments":"摘要只明确说从 toy models 到较大语言模型都观察到更好性能或更快收敛，但没有给出参数量、数据规模和具体指标，所以不能判断收益量级。可以较可信地推断，实验重点应包括 induction 相关合成任务、困惑度或下游 LM 指标、以及收敛曲线比较。若该方法真正有效，最有价值的 ablation 应该是：收益在浅层/窄层模型上是否更明显，以及当模型足够大时，这个结构优势会不会被容量掩盖。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks whether standard decode-only Transformers need so much depth and width to realize induction-like in-context learning, or whether the attention operator itself is making induction unnecessarily hard. The motivation is that induction heads are widely viewed as a core mechanism behind ICL, yet in vanilla attention they typically emerge only through multi-layer composition.","importance":"This is a focused architectural paper with potentially meaningful implications. If the claim holds, part of ICL can be made easier to learn by changing the attention parameterization rather than scaling model depth. That matters for small models, convergence speed, and mechanistic architecture design, though it is still an incremental paradigm-level contribution rather than a new training framework.","technical_approach":"The method is KV shifting attention, an attention variant that shifts keys and values so the model can express prefix-match-then-copy behavior more directly. It does not change the language modeling objective and does not add an external memory; it changes the operator so that the transition structure needed for induction is more explicit. The stated theory claim is that this reduces the depth and width requirements for implementing induction heads compared with standard attention.","experiments":"The abstract only says the method improves performance or accelerates convergence from toy settings to larger language models, with no model sizes, data sizes, or metric values. the Optimal Data Mixture without Training","authors":["Mozhi Zhang","Howe Tissue","Lu Wang","Xipeng Qiu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$29","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.10952","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/ZhangTWQ25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/ZhangTWQ25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/zhang25bu.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：能不能不真正训练语言模型，就先判断一组候选数据源该怎么配比。过去数据混合通常靠经验、少量试训、或 expensive 的 scaling sweep 来找，成本高且迁移性差；作者试图把“数据集”本身向量化，从分布匹配角度直接预测更优 mixture。","importance":"这是典型的数据级工作，对预训练很有现实价值。它如果成立，意义不在于把最优配比做得绝对更好，而在于把找 mixture 的成本从“训练很多次模型”降到“训练一个数据表示器再做推断”；对大规模 pretrain，哪怕只少走几轮试训，节省都很可观。更重要的是，它把数据配比问题从启发式搜索转成了可解释的分布对齐问题。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Domain2Vec：先构造一个 meta-domain 词表，再把任意数据集分解成这些 meta-domain 的线性组合，得到一个 domain vector。作者依赖一个 Distribution Alignment Assumption（DA^2）：训练集和验证集分布越对齐，验证 loss 越低。基于这个假设，问题就变成在候选数据源上找一个 mixture，使其 domain vector 更接近目标验证分布。这个设计的关键取舍是：它放弃了直接模拟完整训练动态，转而用较粗粒度但训练无关的分布表示做代理，因此速度快、可解释，但上限取决于 meta-domain 词表是否足够表达真实数据差异，以及 DA^2 在多任务、多阶段训练里是否稳定成立。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型规模、数据量和数值结果，因此不能报告准确提升幅度。可以确认的是，作者声称在 training-free 设定下能识别更优的数据 mixture，并将 domain vector 用于数据分析与选择。对这类工作，真正需要关注的实验点通常是三类：一是与 random / heuristic mixture、少量 trial training、已有 data selection 方法相比的接近度；二是跨模型规模与跨验证集目标的稳健性；三是 meta-domain 数量和分类器质量对 mixture 推荐结果的敏感性。摘要未提供这些数字，结论应保守看待。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a very practical question: can we choose pretraining data mixtures without actually training the model? Today mixture design is usually driven by heuristics, expensive pilot runs, or repeated scaling sweeps. The authors try to replace that loop with a dataset representation that predicts good mixtures from distribution alignment alone.","importance":"This is a data-centric contribution with clear pretraining relevance. If the approach holds up, the biggest win is not necessarily finding a globally better mixture, but reducing mixture search from many training runs to a training-free inference step. That matters because data mixture tuning is one of the most expensive and least principled parts of large-scale pretraining.","technical_approach":"The core idea is Domain2Vec: represent any dataset as a linear combination of learned meta-domains, producing a domain vector over a shared vocabulary. The method relies on a Distribution Alignment Assumption, namely that lower validation loss is achieved when the training distribution is better aligned with the validation distribution. Under that assumption, mixture selection becomes a vector matching problem rather than a model training problem. The main trade-off is clear: the method gives up direct simulation of optimization and uses a coarse but cheap proxy. That makes it scalable and interpretable, but its ceiling depends on how expressive the meta-domain basis is and how often the alignment assumption survives real multi-stage pretraining settings.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model sizes, dataset sizes, baselines, or exact gains, so there are no trustworthy numbers to report. What can be said is that the authors claim the domain vectors can identify better pretraining mixtures in a training-free way and support broader dataset analysis. For a paper like this, the critical evidence would be how closely its recommended mixtures match those found by actual training, how robust the method is across scales and targets, and how sensitive it is to the number and quality of meta-domains. Those details are not available here.","authors_note":""},"tags":["data-mixture","data-quality","scaling-law","dataset-selection"]},{"id":"2504.16431","title":"Target Concrete Score Matching: A Holistic Framework for Discrete Diffusion","authors":["Ruixiang Zhang","Shuangfei Zhai","Yizhe Zhang","James Thornton","Zijing Ou","Joshua M. Susskind","Navdeep Jaitly"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$2a","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.16431","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/ZhangZ0TOSJ25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/ZhangZ0TOSJ25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/zhang25cw.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：离散 diffusion 模型缺少一个统一、可扩展到预训练和后训练的目标函数。过去 D3PM、SEDD、MDLM 一类方法各自有自己的训练目标，彼此关系不够统一；更麻烦的是，离散 diffusion 在 reward fine-tuning、preference optimization、AR teacher distillation 这些 post-train 场景下工具箱还很弱。","importance":"这是明显的范式级工作，且和 pretrain/posttrain 闭环直接相关。它的重要性不只是“又一个 discrete diffusion loss”，而是试图把离散 diffusion 的训练、偏好微调、奖励优化、从 AR 模型蒸馏，统一到同一个目标里。如果这个统一是干净的，它会显著降低 diffusion LM 生态的碎片化，也让非 AR 生成模型更容易接入今天的对齐与蒸馏基础设施。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Target Concrete Score Matching（TCSM），一个围绕 concrete score 估计构造的统一目标。作者的关键设计不是继续为某一种 corruption process 或某一种参数化单独推损失，而是定义一个更一般的目标，使许多已有离散 diffusion 方法都变成它的特例。更有价值的是，这个目标天然延伸到 post-training：可以用 reward function 或 preference data 做 fine-tuning，也可以从预训练好的 AR 模型蒸馏知识到 diffusion 模型。这样设计有效的原因是，它把训练信号统一成了对离散空间中某种 score/相对概率结构的估计，而不是绑死在 teacher forcing 式 next-token likelihood 上。边界在于：统一目标不等于统一优化难度，离散 diffusion 仍然可能受采样步数、噪声调度和方差控制影响。","experiments":"摘要没有给出模型大小、数据规模、具体 benchmark 和数值提升，因此无法报告精确结果。可以确定的实验主张有三点：第一，TCSM 能直接从数据样本做离散 diffusion 预训练；第二，已有多种离散 diffusion 方法可被还原为它的特例；第三，同一目标还能支持 reward/preference fine-tuning 以及从 AR 模型蒸馏。这类论文最值得关注的实验通常是：和 D3PM/SEDD/MDLM 的同规模比较，post-train 后在 win rate 或 reward 上的改善，以及蒸馏后生成质量/推理成本的变化。摘要未给数字，但问题设定本身很强。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a real gap in discrete diffusion: there is still no single objective that cleanly spans pretraining and post-training. Existing approaches such as D3PM, SEDD, and MDLM use related but fragmented objectives, and discrete diffusion has had a much weaker story than autoregressive models when it comes to reward tuning, preference optimization, or teacher distillation.","importance":"This is a paradigm-level contribution with direct relevance to the pretrain-posttrain loop. The significance is not merely another loss for discrete diffusion. The more important claim is that pretraining, preference or reward fine-tuning, and distillation from autoregressive teachers can all be expressed within one framework. If that unification is technically sound, it lowers fragmentation in diffusion LM research and makes non-AR models easier to integrate into today’s alignment stack.","technical_approach":"The method is Target Concrete Score Matching, a unified objective built around estimating a concrete score in discrete spaces. The key move is not to derive yet another loss tied to one corruption process or one parameterization, but to define a broader objective under which many existing discrete diffusion methods become special cases. More importantly, the same objective extends to post-training: it can use reward functions or preference data, and it can distill knowledge from pretrained autoregressive teachers. The design is compelling because it shifts the supervision target toward score-like relative probability structure rather than teacher-forced next-token likelihood alone. The boundary is that a unified objective does not remove the usual optimization and sampling issues of discrete diffusion, such as step count, schedule design, and variance.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model sizes, dataset scales, benchmarks, or exact gains, so no numeric summary can be trusted. What is clear is that the authors claim three capabilities: direct pretraining of discrete diffusion from samples, recovery of multiple prior methods as special cases, and extension to reward/preference fine-tuning plus AR-teacher distillation. For this kind of paper, the decisive evidence would be same-scale comparisons against D3PM/SEDD/MDLM, post-training gains in reward or preference win rate, and the quality-cost trade-off after distillation. Those details are not available in the abstract.","authors_note":""},"tags":["discrete-diffusion","diffusion-lm","score-matching","training-objective"],"notable_authors":["Shuangfei Zhai","Yizhe While theoretical results show that polynomial-length scratchpads can extend transformers'expressivity from $TC^0$ to $PTIME$, their required length remains poorly understood. Empirical evidence even suggests that transformers need scratchpads even for many problems in $TC^0$, such as Parity or Multiplication, challenging optimistic bounds derived from circuit complexity. In this work, we initiate the study of systematic lower bounds for the number of chain-of-thought steps across different algorithmic problems, in the hard-attention regime. We study a variety of algorithmic problems, and provide bounds that are tight up to logarithmic factors. Overall, these results contribute to emerging understanding of the power and limitations of chain-of-thought reasoning.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.02393","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/BavandpourHR025","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/BavandpourHR025","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/bavandpour25a.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是：在 hard-attention Transformer 中，完成某些算法任务究竟至少需要多长的 Chain-of-Thought/scratchpad，而不是只知道‘有 scratchpad 会更强’。过去理论工作更多给上界，说明多项式长度 scratchpad 可以把表达能力从较弱电路类扩展到 PTIME，但这并没有回答实际中为什么连一些看上去不难的问题也需要显式中间步骤。","importance":"这项工作是 CoT 理论中的基础补全。它的重要性在于给出了系统性的下界视角，告诉我们‘需要 CoT’不是经验现象，而可能是由任务结构决定的。对 pretrain 和推理系统的启发是：如果某类任务在结构上就需要足够长的中间计算，那么单靠更强监督或更好提示词，未必能把短思维链压到极短；推理时 compute budget 与任务难度之间可能存在硬约束。","technical_approach":"作者研究的是 hard-attention 设定下不同算法问题所需的 CoT 步数下界，并且摘要称这些界在对数因子内是紧的。这里 hard attention 可以理解为一种更离散、可分析的 Transformer 变体，便于证明中间步骤数量与可计算问题族之间的关系。和已有工作主要证明‘足够长就能做’不同，这篇论文的关键增量是证明‘不够长就一定做不了’，并覆盖了多个算法问题。这样做有效，因为它把经验上观察到的 Parity、Multiplication 等任务对 scratchpad 的依赖，转化成形式化的复杂度约束。边界也要明确：下界是在 hard-attention 模型下证明的，不能直接等同于现代 soft-attention LLM 的精确能力边界，但它提供了很强的参照系。","experiments":"摘要给出的核心事实是理论性的：他们为一系列算法问题建立了 CoT 步数下界，而且紧到仅差对数因子。没有参数量、数据规模或 benchmark 数字，因为重点不是实证 SOTA。值得关注的不是‘分数提升’，而是问题族之间下界的差异：哪些任务只需很短 scratchpad，哪些任务步数必然随输入长度增长，以及这些结论如何与已有经验结果相呼应。若正文有实验，价值主要在 sanity check——验证理论趋势而非追求绝对性能。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a sharper question than prior expressivity results: for hard-attention Transformers, how many chain-of-thought or scratchpad steps are actually necessary for different algorithmic problems? Earlier theory mostly provided upper bounds, showing that polynomial-length scratchpads can increase expressivity, but that does not explain why even seemingly simple tasks often appear to require explicit intermediate computation in practice.","importance":"This is a foundational contribution to CoT theory. Its importance comes from supplying lower bounds, which turn the empirical observation that some tasks need scratchpads into a structural statement about computational requirements. For pretraining and inference systems, the implication is practical: if certain tasks inherently require a minimum amount of intermediate computation, then better prompting or better supervision alone may not compress reasoning length arbitrarily.","technical_approach":"The paper studies lower bounds on the number of CoT steps required for various algorithmic tasks in the hard-attention regime, and the abstract states that the bounds are tight up to logarithmic factors. Hard attention gives a more discrete and analyzable Transformer model, making it possible to relate reasoning length to problem structure. The main advance over prior work is the shift from 'long enough scratchpads are sufficient' to 'short scratchpads are provably insufficient,' across multiple task families. That is valuable because it formalizes why tasks such as parity or multiplication can resist short-horizon reasoning. The limitation is that these are lower bounds for a stylized model, not exact capability statements about modern soft-attention LLMs.","experiments":"The main results are theoretical rather than empirical: the paper derives lower bounds for a range of algorithmic problems and matches them up to logarithmic factors. Our findings mitigate the risks associated with large-scale distillation by enabling compute-optimal allocation for both the teacher and student to maximize student performance. We provide compute-optimal distillation recipes for two key scenarios: when a teacher already exists, and when a teacher needs training. In settings involving many students or an existing teacher, distillation outperforms supervised learning up to a compute level that scales predictably with student size. Conversely, if only one student is to be distilled and a teacher also requires training, supervised learning is generally preferable. Additionally, our large-scale study of distillation increases our understanding of the process and helps inform experimental design.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.08606","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/BusbridgeSWRLW25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/BusbridgeSWRLW25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/busbridge25a.html","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["distillation","scaling-law","compute-allocation","teacher-student"]},{"id":"2505.07067","title":"Learning curves theory for hierarchically compositional data with power-law distributed features","authors":["Francesco Cagnetta","Hyunmo Kang","Matthieu Wyart"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Recent theories suggest that Neural Scaling Laws arise whenever the task is linearly decomposed into power-law distributed units. Alternatively, scaling laws also emerge when data exhibit a hierarchically compositional structure, as is thought to occur in language and images. To unify these views, we consider classification and next-token prediction tasks based on probabilistic context-free grammars -- probabilistic models that generate data via a hierarchy of production rules. For classification, we show that having power-law distributed production rules results in a power-law learning curve with an exponent depending on the rules' distribution and a large multiplicative constant that depends on the hierarchical structure. By contrast, for next-token prediction, the distribution of production rules controls the local details of the learning curve, but not the exponent describing the large-scale behaviour.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.07067","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/CagnettaKW25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/CagnettaKW25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/cagnetta25a.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["scaling-law","learning-curves","compositionality","theory"]},{"id":"CaiJQC0025","title":"Shortcut-connected Expert Parallelism for Accelerating Mixture of Experts","authors":["Weilin Cai","Juyong Jiang","Le Qin","Junwei Cui","Sunghun Kim","Jiayi Huang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/CaiJQC0025","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/CaiJQC0025","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/cai25c.html","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["moe","expert-parallelism","systems","training-efficiency"]},{"id":"CaiVZOJ25","title":"Attention-Level Speculation","authors":["Jack Cai","Ammar Vora","Randolph Zhang","Mark O&apos;Connor","Mark C. We introduce ACTOR (Activation-Based Training for Over-Refusal Reduction), a robust and compute- and data-efficient training framework that minimizes over-refusals by leveraging internal activation patterns from diverse queries. ACTOR precisely identifies and adjusts the activation components that trigger refusals, providing stronger control over the refusal mechanism. By fine-tuning only a single model layer, ACTOR effectively reduces over-refusals across multiple benchmarks while maintaining the model's ability to handle harmful queries and preserve overall utility.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.04250","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/Dabas0F0025","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/Dabas0F0025","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/dabas25a.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：安全对齐后的 LLM 常出现 over-refusal，对无害请求也过度拒绝，导致模型可用性明显下降。过去常见缓解方法要么继续做全参数对齐微调，要么依赖额外偏好数据和复杂 reward 设计，但这些方法往往会连带削弱安全边界，因此作者聚焦于更细粒度的问题：能否只改动触发拒答的内部表示，而不是整体重写模型行为。","importance":"这是一个 post-train 接口问题，但对 pretrain 研究也有价值，因为它说明对齐副作用可以定位到相对局部的表示子空间，而不一定需要全模型再训练。它的重要性在于给出一种低成本、高控制性的修复思路：如果 refusal 机制在内部激活上是可分离的，那么安全与效用之间的张力就不必总靠大规模数据和 RLHF 重新平衡。","technical_approach":"ACTOR 的核心是 activation-based 的 targeted fine-tuning：先利用多样化查询识别会触发过度拒答的激活成分，再只微调单层去调整这些成分。它不是常规 LoRA/SFT 那种基于输出行为的整体适配，而是直接干预 refusal 相关表示。这样设计有效的原因是，over-refusal 往往不是知识缺失，而是某些安全判别特征被过度泛化；如果能局部压低这些特征，就有机会在不碰其他能力的前提下恢复正常回答。边界也很清楚：如果拒答机制分布在多层且高度纠缠，只调单层的上限会受限。","experiments":"摘要明确给出的事实是：ACTOR 只微调单个模型层，在多个 benchmark 上降低 over-refusal，同时保持对有害查询的处理能力和总体 utility。作者强调其 compute- 和 data-efficient，但未给出所用模型、层位选择、数据规模、具体 benchmark 名称以及提升幅度，因此不能补充数字。实验上最值得关注的点应是：与全参数 SFT 或普通参数高效微调相比，安全保持是否更稳，以及单层干预的迁移性如何。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles over-refusal in aligned LLMs: models reject benign requests too often after safety tuning, hurting usefulness. Standard fixes usually require another round of broad fine-tuning or preference optimization, which can blur safety boundaries, so the authors instead ask whether the refusal mechanism can be adjusted more locally.","importance":"This is a post-training interface contribution with broader relevance to pretraining and alignment. That makes the safety-utility trade-off potentially more controllable and much cheaper to edit.","technical_approach":"ACTOR is a targeted, activation-based fine-tuning method. It identifies activation components associated with refusal across diverse queries and then adjusts only a single layer to reduce excessive refusals. This differs from standard SFT or LoRA-style adaptation that learns from output behavior more globally. The design makes sense because over-refusal is often not a knowledge problem; it is a decision-boundary problem where safety features get overgeneralized. A local representation edit can therefore help without broadly disturbing the model. The limitation is obvious: if refusal behavior is distributed across many layers, a one-layer intervention may have a ceiling.","experiments":"The abstract clearly states that ACTOR fine-tunes only one layer and reduces over-refusal across multiple benchmarks while preserving the handling of harmful queries and overall utility. However, the provided text does not include the base models, datasets, benchmark names, or quantitative gains, so no exact numbers should be reported.","authors_note":""},"tags":["alignment","representation-finetuning","safety","over-refusal"]},{"id":"2505.14826","title":"FisherSFT: Data-Efficient Supervised Fine-Tuning of Language Models Using Information Gain","authors":["Rohan Deb","Kiran Koshy Thekumparampil","Kousha Kalantari","Gaurush Hiranandani","Shoham Sabach","Branislav Kveton"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is a standard approach to adapting large language models (LLMs) to new domains. In this work, we improve the statistical efficiency of SFT by selecting an informative subset of training examples. Specifically, for a fixed budget of training examples, which determines the computational cost of fine-tuning, we determine the most informative ones. The key idea in our method is to select examples that maximize information gain, measured by the Hessian of the log-likelihood of the LLM. We approximate it efficiently by linearizing the LLM at the last layer using multinomial logistic regression models. Our approach is computationally efficient, analyzable, and performs well empirically. We demonstrate this on several problems, and back our claims with both quantitative results and an LLM evaluation.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14826","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/DebTKHSK25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/DebTKHSK25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/deb25a.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：SFT 的算力预算往往由训练样本数决定，但大多数样本并不等价，有些样本对参数更新的信息增益明显更高。过去常见做法是随机采样或基于启发式质量分数筛数据，这些方法简单但和模型当前参数状态脱节，因此数据效率并不理想。","importance":"这是一个 post-train 数据选择方法，但它对 pretrain 也有广泛启发，因为它把“选什么数据最值”写成了近似可计算的信息量问题。重要性在于，它不是又一个经验性的 data scoring，而是试图用 Fisher/Hessian 近似把样本价值和模型不确定性联系起来；如果这条路线稳，预训练和 continual pretrain 的数据配比都可能受益。","technical_approach":"FisherSFT 的核心是：在固定样本预算下，选择能最大化信息增益的训练样本，信息增益由 LLM 对数似然 Hessian 衡量。直接算全模型 Hessian 代价太高，所以作者把 LLM 在最后一层线性化，并用多项逻辑回归近似来高效估计样本信息量。这个设计是一个明确的精度-成本折中：它放弃全模型二阶精确性，换来可分析、可落地的选择器。与基于困惑度或 embedding 覆盖度的筛选相比，它更关注“样本会如何改变参数”，因此更贴近真正的微调目标。","experiments":"摘要能确认的结论是：该方法在多个问题上经验效果良好，并主打 data-efficient SFT。未提供具体基础模型、任务、预算比例、基线方法和提升幅度，因此无法写出量化结果。最值得关注的实验通常会包括：在 10%/20% 数据预算下相对随机采样的收益、最后一层线性化近似与全模型行为的一致性，以及不同域迁移场景下信息增益选择是否稳健。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a simple but important inefficiency in SFT: the compute budget is usually tied to the number of training examples, yet examples are not equally informative. Random sampling and heuristic data scoring are easy to use, but they are only weakly connected to how a specific model would actually update.","importance":"This is a data-selection method for post-training, with wider relevance to pretraining as well. If this works robustly, it could inform not only SFT but also data curation for continual pretraining.","technical_approach":"FisherSFT selects a subset of examples that maximizes information gain under a fixed sample budget, using the Hessian of the log-likelihood as the signal. Since full-model second-order computation is too expensive, the method linearizes the LLM at the last layer and approximates the problem with multinomial logistic regression. This is a deliberate accuracy-cost trade-off: give up full second-order fidelity to get a selector that is efficient, analyzable, and practical. Crucially, such LLMs need to ground their generations in any feedback obtained to reliably achieve the desired outcomes. We propose an end-to-end reinforcement learning method for teaching models to leverage execution feedback in the realm of code synthesis, where state-of-the-art LLMs struggle to improve code iteratively compared to independent sampling. We benchmark on competitive programming tasks, where we achieve new state-of-the art results with both small (8B parameters) and large (70B) models while reducing the amount of samples required by an order of magnitude. Our analysis of inference-time behavior demonstrates that our method produces LLMs that effectively leverage automatic feedback over multiple steps.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.02089","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/GehringZCMCS25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/GehringZCMCS25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/gehring25a.html","category_id":"coding_agent","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：代码 LLM 在多轮编程中拿到执行反馈后，仍然不擅长真正利用反馈来迭代修正。现有模型常能一次性采样出还不错的程序，但在 agent 式多步场景里，面对编译错误、测试失败或运行结果，改进幅度反而有限，说明模型没有学会把环境反馈纳入策略。","importance":"这是一个后训练范式上比较有分量的结果。它的重要性在于把 execution feedback 从推理时提示信息，提升为 RL 中的状态转移与奖励来源，并在 competitive programming 上展示了显著的样本效率增益。对 pretrain 研究的启示是：某些能力，尤其是“根据外部可验证反馈反复修正”的能力，很难靠 next-token 预训练自然长出来，需要显式交互式训练信号补足。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 RLEF，用强化学习把代码生成和执行反馈闭环打通。与把测试结果简单拼进上下文再继续采样不同，这里是端到端训练模型把反馈视为决策依据，在多轮生成中优化最终成功率。它针对的是 code synthesis 的交互性，而不是静态补全。这个设计有效，因为执行反馈是高信息密度、可验证、低歧义的学习信号，能直接指出哪一步策略有问题。代价在于环境回报稀疏、训练成本高，而且代码场景的可执行反馈优势不一定能原样迁移到开放域语言任务。","experiments":"摘要给出了几个关键事实：在 competitive programming 上，RLEF 在 8B 和 70B 模型上都取得了新的 SOTA，并将达到同等效果所需的样本数降低了一个数量级。作者还分析了推理时行为，说明模型确实学会了利用反馈，而不是仅靠更强的独立采样。摘要未给出具体 benchmark 名称和精确分数，但“8B 与 70B 双规模成立 + 样本效率提升 10x”已经说明结论比较稳。值得进一步关注的是：收益主要来自更好的错误定位、测试驱动修复，还是更优的探索策略。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a clear weakness in code LLM agents: they often receive execution feedback but still fail to use it effectively for iterative improvement. Strong one-shot code generation does not automatically translate into strong multi-step problem solving when compilation errors, test failures, and runtime outputs become part of the loop.","importance":"This is a meaningful post-training result because it upgrades execution feedback from a prompt artifact to a training signal inside reinforcement learning. The paper matters beyond code because it shows that some capabilities—especially iterative correction grounded in external, verifiable feedback—are not reliably induced by standard language modeling alone. They may need explicit interactive training.","technical_approach":"The method, RLEF, trains code models end-to-end with reinforcement learning so they can condition on execution feedback across multiple rounds. This is different from simply appending test outputs to the context and sampling again. The policy is optimized for eventual task success in an environment where feedback is part of the state. The reason this works in code is that execution signals are dense in information and objectively checkable. The trade-off is environment cost and reward sparsity, and the setup may not transfer cleanly to open-ended language tasks.","experiments":"The abstract provides several strong facts: on competitive programming, RLEF reaches new state of the art with both 8B and 70B models, and reduces the number of required samples by an order of magnitude. The authors also analyze inference-time behavior and claim the model truly learns to use feedback, rather than benefiting from stronger independent sampling alone. Agrawal"],"institutions":["MIT"],"abstract":"$43","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.12276","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/HanSG025","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/HanSG025","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/han25h.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要回答的核心问题是：ICL 里被反复观察到的 task vector 到底是在预训练/训练中怎样形成的，以及它和最终任务表现之间是不是有可预测的对应关系。过去很多工作只是在训练后做表征分析，证明“模型里有 task vector”，但没有把 task encoding 的形成过程、conditional decoding 的形成过程和 ICL 成功联系成一个动态机制；这篇工作试图把这三件事放到同一个 encoder-decoder 视角里解释。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要在机制层，而不是提出一个更强的新模型。对 pretrain 研究的启示是：ICL 可能不是单纯靠更大的模型或更长的上下文自然涌现，而是依赖两个耦合能力同时长出来——先把上下文里的隐含任务压成可分离表示，再学会基于该表示调用合适的解码算法；这给分析训练动态、设计 probing 指标、甚至构造更有利于 ICL 的训练目标都提供了更具体的抓手。","technical_approach":"作者的方法核心是把 ICL 明确拆成 encoding 和 decoding 两步来研究，而不是把整个前向过程当黑盒。具体做法看起来是：在合成 ICL 任务上训练小型 transformer，跟踪不同训练阶段的隐藏表示，检查模型是否已经把不同 latent task 编成彼此可分的向量，同时观察输出端是否已经学会根据该向量执行条件解码。这个设计的关键选择是用可控的 synthetic task，而不是直接上真实自然语言任务；代价是生态有效性有限，但好处是可以把“任务表征是否可分”“解码算法是否就位”“最终 ICL 精度如何”三者做清晰对应。和已有只做 post-hoc linear probe 的工作相比，它更强调 emergence 的时间顺序和二者耦合关系。","experiments":"从摘要可确认的实验事实是：作者在合成 ICL 任务上分析了一个小型 transformer 的训练动态，并报告了 task encoding 与 decoding 的耦合涌现；随着模型逐步把不同 latent task 编成更清晰、可分离的表示，ICL 表现同步提升。任务示例包括类似“找出句子中的第一个名词”这类潜在规则型任务。摘要还说他们在更真实的 setting 上做了验证，但给出的片段被截断了，因此无法可靠写出模型规模、数据规模、baseline 名称和具体提升数字。这篇论文更像是机制验证，不像以 SOTA 指标为主的工作；若要判断外推价值，重点应看他们是否展示了：1）task encoding 质量与测试任务精度有稳定相关性；2）encoding/decoding 的出现是否存在先后或瓶颈层；3）该相关性是否能跨任务族成立。","authors_note":"MIT；Pulkit Agrawal 是 MIT 方向上较知名的研究者，长期做表征学习、控制与具身智能相关工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a precise question: how task vectors emerge during training and whether their quality actually predicts in-context learning performance. Prior work mostly stopped at post-hoc observations that transformers contain task-like directions in representation space, but did not connect representation formation, conditional decoding, and ICL success into one training-time account.","importance":"This is a mechanism paper, not a headline benchmark paper, and that is exactly why it matters. For pretraining research, it suggests that ICL may depend on two coupled capabilities emerging together: encoding the latent task from demonstrations into a separable internal state, and decoding conditionally using that state. That gives a more actionable lens than the vague claim that ICL simply appears with scale.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to decompose ICL into task encoding and task-conditioned decoding, then study both during training instead of treating the transformer as a black box. The authors train a small transformer on synthetic ICL tasks and track hidden representations over time, asking whether different latent tasks become separable and whether the model simultaneously acquires the decoding algorithm needed for each task. Using synthetic tasks is a deliberate trade-off: it limits realism but makes the latent task identity controllable, so representation quality, decoding behavior, and final task accuracy can be compared directly. Relative to earlier probing-style work, the contribution is the coupled emergence story rather than a static representation analysis.","experiments":"From the abstract, the main empirical result is that task encoding and decoding emerge together during training on synthetic ICL tasks, and ICL performance improves as latent tasks become more distinctly represented. The tasks include rule-like settings such as identifying the first noun in a sentence. The abstract also claims validation beyond the synthetic setup, but the provided text is truncated, so model size, data scale, baselines, and exact gains cannot be stated reliably. The paper should therefore be read mainly for its training-dynamics evidence: whether encoding quality tracks downstream ICL accuracy, whether there is a consistent emergence order, and whether the correlation transfers across task families.","authors_note":"MIT; Pulkit Agrawal is a well-known MIT researcher with representative work in representation learning, control, and embodied reward，可以显式地写出来并直接优化。","importance":"这项工作的意义在 post-training 方法层，而且和 pretrain 的闭环有关。因为偏好优化越来越像基础模型能力释放和行为塑形的主要接口；如果 implicit reward 的便利性带来了偏差、不可控性或不稳定，那么显式 reward 形式可能更便于分析训练信号质量、做 reward shaping，并理解为什么某些偏好算法会 reward hack 或过拟合偏好数据。","technical_approach":"作者提出 Explicit Preference Optimization（EPO），核心思想是绕开 DPO 的隐式奖励构造，直接使用显式 reward 目标进行优化。这里的关键不是‘重新发明 RLHF’，而是找到一种不需要单独训练隐式/显式 reward 冠军，而是：1）在不同偏好数据质量下是否更稳；2）是否减少 reference model 敏感性；3）在 win rate、长度偏置、KL 控制和训练稳定性上是否优于 DPO；4）是否能解释某些 DPO 变体成功只是因为隐式 reward 近似得比较好。若这些点做扎实，这篇论文会比单纯再提一个 preference loss 更有价值。","authors_note":"作者列表含 David Prior low-rank compression often focused on decomposition tricks without tying them to training dynamics.","importance":"It combines mechanism-level explanation with actionable compression guidance by linking low-rank expressiveness to gradient-subspace stabilization (via Hessian analysis). This matters because current reasoning pipelines often assume step-level supervision is inherently easier, which has justified expensive annotation efforts without a clean theoretical basis.","importance":"This is a theory paper with practical consequences for how we collect supervision. Its contribution is not a new training recipe but a correction to a widely held assumption: if outcome supervision is not fundamentally harder in the statistical sense, then process supervision should be treated as a cost-benefit choice rather than a default. For pretrain/post-train work, the implication is that signal granularity may be less important than coverage, bias, and optimization.","technical_approach":"The paper formalizes reinforcement learning for complex reasoning and compares process- and outcome-level supervision through a statistical lens. The central theorem shows that once standard data coverage is assumed, the usual argument against outcome supervision largely disappears. That is useful because it separates two issues that are often conflated: statistical identifiability versus optimization and exploration. Instead, it changes the inference-time form of the expert itself. That is a useful systems-architecture idea: the bottleneck of MoE deployment may be addressed by reparameterizing experts, not only by serving heuristics.","technical_approach":"MoLE keeps experts as FFNs during training, but reparameterizes them into lookup tables before inference. In this work, we propose to frame this problem in a causal paradigm, providing the rich toolbox of causality to identify the persistent challenges, such as causal misidentification, preference heterogeneity, and confounding due to user-specific factors. Inheriting from the literature of causal inference, we identify key assumptions necessary for reliable generalisation and contrast them with common data collection practices. We illustrate failure modes of naive reward models and demonstrate how causally-inspired approaches can improve model robustness. 信号。","technical_approach":"方法框架将 BT 的二元比较推广到 K 级序数反馈，核心是提出一个“边际无偏性条件”（marginal unbiasedness）来刻画在什么假设下，用序数标签训练得到的 RM 仍能无偏地恢复潜在效用排序；在实现上，它允许把 tie、slightly better settings.","importance":"It is a direct statistical upgrade to reward modeling: it generalizes binary preference learning to arbitrary-granularity ordinal feedback and provides an unbiasedness condition, enabling more of the crowd’s graded information to translate into a trainable RM signal.","technical_approach":"The framework extends BT-style modeling from binary to K-level ordinal labels, centered on a “marginal unbiasedness” condition that specifies when learning from ordinal feedback can still recover the underlying utility ordering without bias; practically, it incorporates Standard scaling mostly increases hidden width, heads, and FFN size, while the residual path remains a narrow communication bottleneck across layers.","importance":"This is an architecture-level contribution with direct pretraining relevance because it targets representational bandwidth, not just parameter count. If the hypothesis is right, some scaling limits in Transformers may come from information congestion in the residual channel rather than insufficient compute inside attention or MLP blocks.","technical_approach":"From the title, Residual Matrix Transformers replace or augment the usual vector residual stream with a matrix-like residual representation. The key difference from a standard Transformer is that more information can be carried across layers, potentially preserving multiple subspaces or feature groups without forcing them to compete within a single vector. This could help when layer-local computation is rich but cross-layer transmission is lossy. The cost is clear: more memory traffic, more implementation complexity, and possibly worse hardware efficiency.","experiments":"The critical experiments should compare scaling behavior under matched parameters or matched FLOPs, asking whether matrix residuals improve loss, downstream metrics, or optimization stability. Mechanistic Interpretations: An Axiomatic Approach","authors":["Nils Palumbo","Ravi Mangal","Zifan Wang","Saranya Vijayakumar","Corina S. Pasareanu","Somesh Jha"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Mechanistic interpretability aims to reverse engineer the computation performed by a neural network in terms of its internal components. Although there is a growing body of research on mechanistic interpretation of neural networks, the notion of a mechanistic interpretation itself is often ad-hoc. Inspired by the notion of abstract interpretation from the program analysis literature that aims to develop approximate semantics for programs, we give a set of axioms that formally characterize a mechanistic interpretation as a description that approximately captures the semantics of the neural network under analysis in a compositional manner. We demonstrate the applicability of these axioms for validating mechanistic interpretations on an existing, well-known interpretability study as well as on a new case study involving a Transformer-based model trained to solve the well-known 2-SAT problem.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.13594","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/PalumboM0VPJ25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/PalumboM0VPJ25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/palumbo25a.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：mechanistic interpretability 里“什么算一个有效解释”一直缺少统一标准，导致很多解释看起来合理，但其实难验证、难比较、也难复用。过去这类工作常靠案例说服和人工直觉支撑，现在模型越来越大，这种做法的上限已经很明显。","importance":"它的重要性在于给 interpretability 提供了评估框架，而不是再提一个新的解释算法。对 pretrain 研究的意义是间接但不小：如果没有可验证的解释标准，我们很难把机制分析真正变成训练反馈信号、架构诊断工具或 scaling 规律研究的一部分；这篇工作试图把 mechanistic interpretation 从“会讲故事”推进到“可检验的近似语义描述”。","technical_approach":"作者借用了程序分析里的 abstract interpretation 思路，提出一组公理来形式化“机械解释”应满足什么条件。核心点有两个：第一，解释不是任意可视化，而是对神经网络语义的近似描述；第二，这个描述必须具备组合性，也就是局部组件的解释能拼成整体计算的解释。这样的设计是在给解释设边界：如果一个解释不能近似语义，或者不能跨层、跨模块组合，它就更像 post-hoc 叙事而不是机制模型。与常见 interpretability 工作不同，这篇论文的重点不是找 circuit，而是验证 circuit 解释是否站得住。","experiments":"摘要说明作者把这套公理用在一个已有、知名的 interpretability 案例上做验证，并且还展示了进一步应用，但截断后缺少细节。已知的关键事实是：论文有实证部分，不是纯理论；它应该在现有解释案例上检验哪些解释满足公理、哪些不满足。摘要没有给模型规模、任务、评价指标或定量结果，因此更适合把它看成评估准则论文，而不是 benchmark 冲榜论文。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a basic weakness in mechanistic interpretability: the field still lacks a principled notion of what counts as a valid interpretation. Many interpretations are persuasive case studies, but without a standard they are hard to validate, compare, or use operationally.","importance":"Its contribution is primarily evaluative and foundational rather than algorithmic. This matters for pretraining-adjacent research because mechanism analysis only becomes useful as a debugging or design tool if interpretations can be checked as approximate semantic descriptions, not just narrated after the fact.","technical_approach":"The authors draw on abstract interpretation from program analysis and propose axioms that a mechanistic interpretation should satisfy. The key move is to treat an interpretation as an approximate, compositional description of the network’s semantics: it must capture meaning well enough to be useful, and it must compose across internal components rather than living as a one-off story about isolated neurons or heads. This shifts the goal from discovering circuits per se to validating whether a proposed circuit-level explanation actually has semantic content.","experiments":"The abstract states that the axioms are applied to validate at least one existing, well-known interpretability study, and likely additional examples, though the provided text is truncated. The important fact is that this is not purely formal work; it includes empirical validation on prior interpretations. No model sizes, tasks, or quantitative metrics are given in the provided abstract, so it is best read as a framework for evaluating interpretability claims rather than a benchmark-driven paper.","authors_note":""},"tags":["mechanistic-interpretability","evaluation","theory","circuits"]},{"id":"2410.07432","title":"Can Transformers Reason Logically? A Study in SAT Solving","authors":["Leyan Pan","Vijay Ganesh","Jacob D. Abernethy","Chris Esposo","Wenke Lee"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$52","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.07432","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/Pan0AEL25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/Pan0AEL25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/pan25d.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要回答的是：decoder-only Transformer 到底能不能做严格意义上的逻辑推理，而不只是看起来会“模仿推理文本”。这个问题过去常被经验结果模糊带过，但 SAT 是一个足够干净的测试床，因为它有清晰的算法、正确性定义和推理轨迹。","importance":"它的重要性偏范式澄清和能力边界刻画，而不是直接给 pretrain 带来新算法。对基座研究的价值在于，它把“Transformer 能否学会算法式推理”拆成了两个层次：表示能力上能不能实现，以及训练上能不能从轨迹中学到；这比单纯看 benchmark 分数更接近我们关心的 reasoning 机制问题。","technical_approach":"作者先从构造角度证明 decoder-only Transformer 在非一致计算模型下可以决定 3-SAT，并且可以通过 CoT 形式实现回溯和演绎，其执行轨迹与 DPLL 算法可做 trace equivalence 对齐。这个部分说明的是“存在性”：架构容量足够，不是天生做不到。然后作者把这个构造落成 PyTorch 模型和一个叫 PARAT 的工具，用来检查实现正确性与性质。最后再研究“可训练性”：不是手工编程 Transformer，而是让模型直接从算法轨迹，也就是 reasoning paths，去学习 SAT 求解过程。这个三段式设计很合理，因为它区分了架构可表达、人工构造可运行、以及梯度训练可获得三件本来就不同的事。","experiments":"从摘要可确认的实验重点不是传统 NLP 指标，而是验证构造模型的正确性，以及测试模型能否从 DPLL 风格轨迹中学到 SAT 求解。作者明确实现了一个 PyTorch 版本和配套工具 PARAT。摘要没有给出 SAT 规模、变量数、训练数据量、成功率或泛化区间，因此无法判断学习到的是完整算法还是局部模式拟合。即便如此，这篇工作的实验价值依然在于把“证明可行”和“训练能否做到”放到同一框架下比较。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a sharper question than most reasoning benchmarks do: can decoder-only Transformers perform logical reasoning in a formal sense, rather than merely imitate reasoning text? SAT is a good test bed because the task has clean algorithmic structure, exact correctness, and explicit reasoning traces.","importance":"This is more of a capability-boundary and paradigm-clarification paper than a new pretraining method. Its value for foundation-model research is that it separates three questions that are often conflated: whether the architecture can represent the algorithm, whether a correct Transformer implementation can be constructed, and whether gradient training can actually recover that behavior from traces.","technical_approach":"The authors first give a constructive proof that decoder-only Transformers can decide 3-SAT in a non-uniform computation model, using Chain-of-Thought to implement backtracking and deduction, with trace equivalence to DPLL. That establishes representational possibility. They then instantiate the construction as a PyTorch model and provide a tool, PARAT, to empirically verify correctness and inspect properties. Finally, they study learnability by training models directly on algorithmic traces instead of hard-coding the solver. This progression is well designed because expressivity, constructive implementation, and trainability are not the same claim.","experiments":"The experiments, as described in the abstract, focus on validating the constructed Transformer solver and probing whether models can learn SAT solving from DPLL-like traces. We consider generic inference time objectives with $k$ samples, with a focus on pass@$k$ and majority voting as two main applications. With language model training on reasoning datasets, we showcase the performance trade-off enabled by training with such objectives. When training on code generation tasks, we show that the approach significantly improves pass@$k$ objectives compared to the baseline method.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.19595","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/TangZSM25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/TangZSM25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/tang25o.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是一个经常被忽略的问题：训练目标通常优化单样本 next-token likelihood，但实际使用时我们常靠多次采样、pass@k 或多数投票拿最终结果，导致 train-test objective 不一致。过去大家更多在 inference 端调采样策略，这篇工作则反过来问：能不能把 inference-time 目标直接写进训练里。","importance":"这是一个很值得重视的 post-train / objective design 方向。它对 pretrain 和 reasoning 训练的启示是，模型能力并不只取决于单次输出质量，还取决于候选分布是否适合被 test-time selection 利用；如果目标是 pass@k 或 self-consistency，训练时就应该显式塑造这种分布，而不是默认 MLE 足够。","technical_approach":"作者用强化学习来直接优化 inference-time objective，重点覆盖 pass@k 和 majority voting 这两类 k-sample 目标。方法的核心不是再提高单样本 token-level logprob，而是优化‘采 k 次后至少一次对’或‘多样本聚合后更对’这类最终使用指标。这样设计有效，是因为这些目标要求模型在候选集合层面有更好的覆盖、多样性和校准，而纯 MLE 更偏向压高平均似然，不一定提升 best-of-k 表现。和常规 RLHF 的区别在于，这里 reward 面向算法式推理/采样聚合结果，而非人类偏好。","experiments":"摘要明确说在 reasoning 数据集训练时展示了不同 inference-time objective 间的性能 trade-off，并在代码生成任务上显著提升 pass@k，但没有给出模型大小、数据规模、具体 baseline、任务集和增益数字。可以可靠提炼的结论是：显式优化 k-sample 指标会改变模型的输出分布，从而改善实际推理时的聚合结果；若看全文，重点应看 pass@1 是否受损、不同 k 下的最优策略是否一致，以及 majority voting 改善是否来自答案正确率提升还是样本相关性下降。","authors_note":"已知作者包括 Gabriel Synnaeve、Rémi Munos。机构信息无法从给定内容完整确认。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a training-serving mismatch that is easy to overlook: models are typically trained with single-sample likelihood objectives, yet deployed with multi-sample procedures such as pass@k and majority voting. The main lesson for pretraining and reasoning work is that model usefulness depends not only on one-shot quality, but also on whether its output distribution is well shaped for test-time selection and aggregation. If pass@k or self-consistency is how the model is actually used, MLE alone is not the right target.","technical_approach":"The method uses reinforcement learning to optimize generic inference-time objectives, with pass@k and majority voting as the main cases. Instead of improving token-level likelihood for a single sample, it optimizes the final utility of a set of k samples, such as getting at least one correct answer or improving the aggregate vote. A common approach is to raise the decoding temperature. In this work, we investigate this approach through a simplistic yet common case to provide insights into why decreasing temperature can improve quality (Precision), while increasing it often fails to boost coverage (Recall). Our analysis reveals that for a model to be effectively tunable through temperature adjustments, it must be trained toward coverage. To address this, we propose rethinking loss functions in language models by leveraging the Precision-Recall framework. Our results demonstrate that this approach achieves a substantially better trade-off between Precision and Recall than merely combining negative log-likelihood training with temperature scaling. To address these challenges, we propose RLTHF, a human-AI hybrid framework that combines LLM-based initial alignment with selective human annotations to achieve full-human annotation alignment with minimal effort. RLTHF identifies hard-to-annotate samples mislabeled by LLMs using a reward model's reward distribution and iteratively enhances alignment by integrating strategic human corrections while leveraging LLM's correctly labeled samples. Evaluations on HH-RLHF and TL;DR datasets show that RLTHF reaches full-human annotation-level alignment with only 6-7% of the human annotation effort. Furthermore, models trained on RLTHF's curated datasets for downstream tasks outperform those trained on fully human-annotated datasets, underscoring the effectiveness of RLTHF.","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.13417","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/XuCKASLC25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/XuCKASLC25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/xu25c.html","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["rlhf","human-feedback","alignment","data-efficiency"]},{"id":"2502.20332","title":"Emergent Symbolic Mechanisms Support Abstract Reasoning in Large Language Models","authors":["Yukang Yang","Declan Campbell","Kaixuan Huang","Mengdi Wang","Jonathan D. Cohen","Taylor Whittington Webb"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$62","venue":"ICML","year":2025,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.20332","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/icml/YangCHW0W25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/icml/YangCHW0W25","_ee":"https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/yang25c.html","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心结论是：LLM 的抽象推理并不只是模糊统计拟合，内部会自发形成一套近似符号式的机制，用来把表面 token 映射成抽象变量、在变量上做归纳，再把结果检索回具体 token。它要解决的是围绕“LLM 是否真的依赖结构化机制推理”的争论，并试图给出更细的层级化机制证据。","importance":"这是机制分析工作，但对 pretrain 很有价值，因为它把抽象推理能力与特定 attention head 功能分解对应起来。其意义不在刷新 benchmark，而在于提供了一个可操作的解释框架：如果抽象推理依赖 symbol abstraction heads、symbolic induction heads 和 retrieval heads 这样的分工，那么训练数据、架构深度以及 attention 设计都可能影响这套机制是否出现、何时出现、出现后有多稳。","technical_approach":"方法上，作者通过内部表征与头功能分析，识别出一个三阶段 emergent symbolic architecture。早期层的 symbol abstraction heads 依据 token 之间关系把具体输入压成抽象变量；中间层的 symbolic induction heads 在这些变量上做序列归纳；后期层的 retrieval heads 再把抽象关系映射回具体 token 预测。与很多只证明“某些头重要”的 mechanistic interpretability 工作不同，这篇论文试图给出端到端功能链条，而不是孤立组件。这个设计之所以有解释力，是因为它把抽象、规则迁移和最终生成连接成一条可定位的计算路径。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型规模、任务集或定量效果，但可以确认作者研究的是 abstract reasoning，并且给出了支持三类机制分工的证据。最关键的实验应包括：跨层 patching/ablation 验证三阶段缺一不可；在关系变换或变量替换任务上检查这些头是否保持功能稳定；以及这种符号机制随模型规模或训练程度是否出现涌现阈值。没有数字，不能判断其结论覆盖面有多广，但如果这些控制实验充分，这会是一篇机制上很有分量的论文。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The central claim is that abstract reasoning in LLMs is supported by an emergent symbolic-like mechanism rather than only diffuse statistical pattern matching. The paper addresses the ongoing debate over whether robust reasoning in LLMs depends on structured internal computations, and it tries to provide layer-wise mechanistic evidence rather than behavioral hints.","importance":"This is a mechanistic interpretability paper with real relevance to pretraining. Its value is not in benchmark gains, but in decomposing abstract reasoning into interpretable attention-head functions. If reasoning depends on a pipeline of symbol abstraction, symbolic induction, and retrieval, then data mixture, depth, and attention design may directly affect whether this machinery emerges and how robust it is.","technical_approach":"The paper identifies a three-stage emergent symbolic architecture through internal representation and head-function analysis. Early-layer symbol abstraction heads map concrete tokens into abstract variables based on relations among tokens. Mid-layer symbolic induction heads perform sequence induction over these variables. Late-layer retrieval heads map the abstract result back to the correct token prediction. Unlike mechanistic work that only highlights isolated important heads, this paper proposes a functional chain spanning abstraction, rule application, and output recovery.","experiments":"The abstract gives no model sizes, task suites, or quantitative effect sizes, but it clearly studies abstract reasoning and claims evidence for a three-part mechanism. Two different mechanisms have been proposed to explain ICL: induction heads that find and copy relevant tokens, and function vector (FV) heads whose activations compute a latent encoding of the ICL task. To better understand which of the two distinct mechanisms drives ICL, we study and compare induction heads and FV heads in 12 language models. Through detailed ablations, we discover that few-shot ICL performance depends primarily on FV heads, especially in larger models. In addition, we uncover that FV and induction heads are connected: many FV heads start as induction heads during training before transitioning to the FV mechanism. This leads us to speculate that induction facilitates learning the more complex FV mechanism that ultimately drives Its value is that it turns ICL emergence into an analyzable training-dynamics problem and ties observable loss transitions to fixed points and parameterization choices; that is useful if you care about when ICL should emerge, what sudden loss drops mean, and why architecture details may matter for capability formation.","technical_approach":"The method is an analytical study of gradient descent on multi-head linear self-attention trained for in-context linear regression. The authors compare two parameterizations: a merged key-query matrix often used in theory, and separate key and query matrices that are closer to practical transformers. For the merged case, they show two fixed points and a single sharp loss drop, and derive a closed-form time course; the message is that ICL can emerge as a phase-transition-like event rather than smooth accumulation. For the separate case, training becomes multi-stage, suggesting that added parameter freedom changes the route by which the model discovers the in-context algorithm.","experiments":"Based on the available abstract, the evidence is primarily theory plus controlled numerical verification rather than large-scale LM pretraining. Existing sparse attention methods usually depend on model-specific attention patterns, require adaptation, or fail to generalize across architectures, which makes them hard to use as a drop-in optimization.","importance":"This is primarily a systems contribution, but it also matters for model research because it tests whether attention sparsity can be estimated online rather than baked into architecture or training. If that works broadly, it informs long-context serving and may also feed back into how we think about attention importance during training and distillation.","technical_approach":"The method, SpargeAttn, is a universal sparse-and-quantized attention module with a two-stage online filter. Stage one cheaply predicts which attention entries are likely large enough to matter, removing most candidates at low cost. Stage two refines the selection to avoid quality loss from false negatives. Relative to fixed local windows, block sparsity, or model-specific sparse patterns, the main design choice is input-dependent filtering at inference time. The obvious trade-off is overhead: the filtering only helps if the prediction is accurate enough and the resulting sparsity is high enough to beat the cost of the filter itself.","experiments":"The abstract gives no concrete model sizes, context lengths, speedups, quality deltas, or baseline numbers, so there is no reliable quantitative summary to provide. The decisive evidence for a paper like this would be wall-clock gains across architectures, break-even points across sequence lengths, and trade-off curves versus FlashAttention-style dense kernels and prior sparse baselines. Otherwise the nominal bit savings are lost to dequantization overhead. 分解成两个可扩展部件：候选生成质量和验证准确率，并分析两者如何随 sample 数增长共同提升。与常见做法相比，它刻意舍弃树式扩展、过程监督和人工设计的 verifier，目的是隔离出“规模化验证”本身的收益；论文特别强调一种 implicit scaling 现象，即候选越多，越容易出现明显正确和明显错误的对比样本，从而让同一个 verifier Prior work often moved quickly to tree search, process supervision, or external verifiers, implicitly assuming that naive sample-and-rerank saturates early. This work argues that the ceiling is higher than expected, partly because verification itself gets easier when the candidate pool is larger.","importance":"This is a scaling-law style contribution for inference-time compute rather than a new training algorithm. Its relevance to pretrain research is that it reframes what a strong base model should provide: not only next-token quality, but also enough diversity and enough internal judgment for cheap search to work. That has direct implications for how we value pretraining decisions that improve calibratability and self-verification, and when heavy post-training infrastructure is actually necessary.","technical_approach":"The method is intentionally minimal: generate many candidates by random sampling, then ask the model to directly verify or choose the best one. Unlike more elaborate systems, it avoids process reward models, handcrafted search trees, and specialized judges, so the observed gains can be attributed to scaling verification itself. The key mechanism is what the authors call implicit scaling: with more samples, the pool contains clearer contrasts, making the same verifier more likely to identify the correct answer.","experiments":"The headline empirical result is that this minimalist setup lifts Gemini v1.5 Pro above o1-Preview on popular reasoning benchmarks. The most useful empirical takeaway is not just that accuracy improves with more samples, but that verifier accuracy also improves as the pool grows. That means the gain is not only from a better chance of sampling a good answer, but also from easier selection. The limitation is equally clear: if the model's self-evaluation is systematically miscalibrated, extra samples mainly add SFT: even with supervised data, the objective is still generative next-token likelihood, so it does not explicitly teach the model to distinguish better responses from worse ones. The standard fix is a second-stage preference optimization step with human preferences or a reward model. This work asks whether discriminative learning can be brought directly into fine-tuning and remove that dependency.","importance":"This is a post-training method contribution with broader implications for the pretrain-to-posttrain interface. If it works well, it weakens the assumption that alignment must rely on a separate reward modeling stack. That matters operationally because preference data and reward models are expensive, brittle, and often domain-specific. A stronger discriminative fine-tuning stage could simplify alignment pipelines and make customization more accessible.","technical_approach":"The method, Discriminative Fine-Tuning, modifies the training objective of a generative LM so the model learns not just to imitate a target response but to favor better responses over worse ones. It is not a separate reward model and not an RL stage. Compared with vanilla SFT, it adds explicit discriminative pressure. Compared with DPO or RLHF-style methods, it aims to do so without human preference labels or an external reward signal. The intuition is straightforward: many alignment failures are ranking failures, not pure generation failures, and likelihood alone is a weak proxy for ranking quality.","experiments":"From the abstract, the empirical claim is that DFT improves over standard SFT while avoiding reward models and human preference data.
